channel.title: Real Life on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Real Life on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Getting Older
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about getting older.

I know this is going to be a fun Friday so I’m going to keep it light, but I’m just going to talk to you about what getting older feels like, because I’ve gone from being the absolute youngest person on a gamedev team — because I started when I was 16 — to now being the oldest at 60. Now I don’t want to talk about agism. I have a whole video about that, so I don’t need to repeat all that here. You probably do want to watch it. I’ll link it in the description, because what surprises me the most about agism is we’re all getting older And when I watch someone be agist, I’m like you know you’re going to be in that group that you’re making fun of. And pretty soon some of you, in fact one person I’m like you’re in that group already. But I think it was more of a. I think they thought if they fought against the older person. They were fighting against their old, own aging.

item.title: It's OK To Change Your Mind
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about that.

It’s okay for you to change your mind. I’m wondering if this is going to be part of my series. It’s okay to, since I did one a few weeks ago about it’s okay to be proud, Had some very interesting comments from people who really took that as like: hey, you, want to be arrogant, You’re? You know, it’s that pride goeth before the fall. There were a lot of comments like that, Not what I meant, but okay, We’ll see how this one goes on. It’s okay to change your mind.

item.title: Stories I Won’t Tell
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about stories I won’t tell on this channel.

Let me get into why Figured this is a kind of funnish Friday video. First of all, about half of my channel’s content comes from questions that you guys ask. And you guys ask really good questions about games and also about other things like “Hey Tim, why do you think we haven’t detected a magnetic monopole” Or “Hey Tim, how did you develop your keen eye for fashion”. However, I’ve had to talk about questions that I can’t or won’t answer. I made an entire video about comments that I skip.

item.title: Being Real
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about being real.

I just had a friend say that he likes to watch my channel because I’m real And he says, you know, he knows me in real life and that I’m not doing an act for the channel, that I’m just being me telling my stories. And he said he can see it’s me, It’s that the channel is the same passion and gesticulations, and forgetting the names of people and games and techniques like subsurface scattering — that I do in real life. That’s me in real life too, so it was interesting because I at first, when he said this, though, I scratched my head over it because I’m doing a podcast here. Effectively, this is a podcast and that’s what everybody does, right. And he went on to explain. He goes “Look.

item.title: Christmas. Christmas Never Changes.
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Christmas.

Christmas never changes, Except it kind of does. For me, recording this, it’s the day after Thanksgiving here in the United States. So I am full of ham, not turkey. I don’t like turkey. I never really liked turkey. I thought it was dry and kind of tasted off. But a few years ago I miscooked the turkey and I got food poisoning and I’m like: why am I doing this? I don’t even like this, meat. So now I make ham, I make a lot of ham and I eat the ham and I love ham.

item.title: Cain On Games Update
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me. Tim Decided to do a Cain on Games update because, as you can see, things have changed, Lots of changes actually. So I I thought I’d spend a fun Friday walking through the changes so you guys can know what’s going on. First off, I have moved back to Southern California. I did that a little over a week ago my time, but remember I have a big queue — It’s like 6 weeks long, I think — on this channel. So, from your point of view, you kept seeing a bunch of videos that I filmed up in Seattle. Although you did see a video on Arcanum magic design that popped up in this place, These shelves had nothing in it I hadn’t unpacked It’s because someone who is a super mutant paid for a priority video and wanted to know about Arcanum magic design. So I dropped what I was doing and I talked about Arcanum, magic design, and colleges and magic tech and all that.

item.title: Lessons As An Old Person
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to share lessons I’ve learned as an old person.

I’m going to put this up on a Friday. I’m not sure if it counts as a fun Friday, but it’s not as much game development as it’s lessons I’ve learned or have come to learn now that I’m older And since all I’ve ever done is game development, it’s game development stories Context. I turned 60 a few weeks ago. Many of you gave me happy birthday wishes. Thank you, Um.

item.title: Passivity
item.description:
Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about passivity.

What I mean by that is people who work with zero passion about it. They just go with the flow. They don’t care very much about one way or the other. They don’t care about a particular feature or game element. Of course I’m talking about this in the game industry, but bear with me, I have examples of passivity from everything.

item.title: Early Morning Workers
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about early morning workers.

It’s my fun Friday video. I’m talking to you, my fellow early morning people, because I know you don’t get the kudos that you deserve. Everyone’s talking about the people who work late. Everyone likes to talk about how they burn the midnight oil. Nobody talks about my peeps, who come in at 6:00 or 7:00 am and start working. So I wanted to talk about that.

item.title: Maps
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about maps.

I’ve never really talked about maps before because I don’t really make the world maps that you see in my games, but I wanted to talk about them because it’s a fun Friday and I do have a reason for wanting to talk about maps, which I will get to in just a second. But I wanted to kind of do my kind of rambling preamble stuff. I love maps. I’ve always loved maps, Even as a kid. My room was full of maps. I had maps that I would get from when our family would take car trips and I. We would stop at a gas station and they would give you maps of the area. It might have been a road map. It might have been like: here’s a map to beautiful Laurier Ca Caverns, which is someplace we went because we all really wanted to see the rock formations that looked like eggs and bacon. I had theme park maps, there was a theme park in Virginia called King’s Dominion that we went to several times, but my family also went to Disney World a couple times and I had maps from th those places and I would just put them up on the wall and even had a globe. I I was one of those kids who liked looking at a globe, so I had a globe in my room.

item.title: 3D Printing
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about 3D printing.

It’s a fun Friday. I thought I would show you one of my hobbies which, as you will see, even though it was 3D printing, I didn’t stray very far from gaming. Now you saw some of these already in my cabinets. So if you watch the videos which I’ll link below, where I go through everything in these cabinets, you’ve already seen a few of them, but I dug up all of them because I wanted to show what I did when, a few years ago, for Christmas, Robert got me a 3D printer And I’d always wanted to play around with it and I wanted to see how they worked. So the first thing I did when I got it is I set it up, uh, strung in some — and this according to my notes — I wrote it down blue filament and I ran a test print And let me just tell you it was really loud: [Music]. It’s making something called a test spiral. Estimated completion: I hope that’s 2 minutes, but it might be 2 hours. [Music].

item.title: I'm Not A Typical Gamer
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how I’m not a typical gamer.

I know some of you are going “Duh, Tim, you’re not a typical anything”. You ever do anything and immediately regret doing it that was that. I saw an article this morning. I was browsing news sites and the article said that most players now on Steam — so the majority of players on Steam — buy games they don’t play. I was like what? And so I looked into the article and apparently there’s a lot of people out there that just collect games.

item.title: To My Fellow Game Developers
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk to my fellow game developers.

I know this is Friday. This is not going to be my typical fun Friday video. It’s not sad, It’s just a little more serious. And also I’m not talking about the game development process. What I what I want to do is talk to my fellow game developers — developers, - about kind of mindset. I don’t want to talk about game development process. I don’t want to give you ideas for games or tips on how to organize your design or smooth out the development process.

item.title: Silver Linings
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about silver linings.

This is something I’ve been thinking about lately, just situations where it seemed tough. But then I found a better way of thinking about the situation. I kind of reframed it and it was a better way to approach it What made me think of this is I did a video recently — for me, but probably a while back for when this will post — about doing a hypothetical game jam. I’ll do a link below — And during that I pointed out that if I was in a game jam where I had a limited amount of time, I already know what I’m good at and plus I know a bunch of techniques. So I pointed out that if I was going to do that game jam, I would not try to do a game that had a lot of art requirements or a lot of UI, because I’m not very good at making art and I know UI is in a tremendous time sync.

item.title: "Right Mistake-Making"
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about right mistakemaking.

It’s a fun Friday, so I kind of wanted to dive into something that recently occurred to me while I was rereading my favorite book, Lord of Light by Roger Zilasny. I have a video about how it’s one of my most influential books and how I wanted to make an RPG out of it years ago. But what I wanted to do now is read a passage to you from page 253 of the Eastn Press edition. I noticed I have three copies of the book and they’re all have slightly different page numbers.

item.title: Channel Second Anniversary!
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about this Channel’s second anniversary.

It’s a fun Friday, it’s April 25th- 2025, and this Sunday, April 27th, is the 2year anniversary of this channel. I think I’ve mentioned. I thought I’d start it, do a few dozen videos, have a few hundred people watch them and be done. That hasn’t happened, so let’s walk through that, because it’s very surprising.

item.title: A Day In The Life Of Moon Man
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about a day in the life of moonman.

It’s fun. Friday I was digging through some older photos I had that were industry related, and I have a whole Trove of obsidian ones, and there was one I found that was called day in the life of moonman and it was something I did back in 2019 and I think I sent it out to the team. Just did it for fun, being goofy, so I thought I’d share it here. So let’s get started. In [Music] 2019.

item.title: My Bookshelves
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to talk about these bookshelves.

It’s fun. Friday, it’s daylight, I’m still snowed in. Let’s do this.

The latest person to ask about this was blood mime, who asked: hi, Tim, my partner is interested in a Showcase of the items and books on your black shelves. She has a large collection of books, but, based on what we’re able to see, you have an interesting assortment of other items as well.

item.title: Raising Cain
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to tell you the story of Raising Cane, or how I went from being a baby to the gorgeous super hunk you see before you today.

Hold all comments till the end. I see you leaning forward, wanting to type. Just stop, okay.

I’ve mentioned that I have thousands of family photos digitized. On more than one occasion someone in my family has contacted me and said: hey, can you send me all the pictures of Grandma Kaine or can you send me all the pictures of this cousin? And I’ve gone through all thousands of photos looking for the ones with those people in them. So eventually, one day, I realized that storage of these photos was Tiny. They’re digitized photos that are often, you know, tiny little, you know snapshots. So what I did was I made folders for all of my siblings, for my parents, for my grandparents, for cousins and I for any photo. I would make as a as many copies of it as there. Were people in the photo and then I put those f photos into all the folders for those people. So whenever asks me, hey, can you show me pictures of your sister, I have all of them in there, right there. These are all the photos I have. Because of that I took my own photos and because I knew what age I was in them, I made them Chron olical. How lucky for you, because now I’m going to show you pictures of me from my pupil beginnings of a year, age zero, all the way up to when I graduated from high school, and you can see where I came from.

item.title: Changes 2024
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about changes — well, changes as of 2024.

If I’m figuring this out correctly, because I do these early, this particular video will go up on at the very last day of 2024, so it’s New Year’s Eve. There were a lot of changes this year: political ones, social ones, on cultural ones. A lot of people want these changes to stop. They want things to go back to how they were growing up. I was used to hearing this from older people. Now I’m hearing it from younger people.

item.title: Game Lessons From Collecting Fragrances
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to talk about game lessons from collecting fragrances.

It’s fun. Friday I’ve talked about chocolate, talked about dice, so now I’m going to talk about fragrances, something I collect a lot of, and I will show you in a few seconds the entire collection. Little history: I had a few like aftershaves and stuff in college — all of them were gifts. A friend gave me some gray flannel which I really loved, and my stepmother gave me a like a sampler of different things and they were Hit or Miss, but there was a 4711 in there and I remember thinking this is really good.

item.title: The Dog
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to talk about the dog.

By popular demand, many people have asked to see the dog. I’ve done shorts of the dog. I put a whole playlist of them in the description, but today it’s fun. Friday I’m going to talk about the dog. Um, I’m just going to talk about her and throw up pictures and videos as appropriate. I’ve got literally hundreds of photos of her, so let’s start at the beginning.

item.title: A Real Life Fallout Story With Lasers
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to tell you a real life Fallout story with lasers.

Now, this is something it just happened last month, just a few weeks old, and during this story I will say the phrase: she used her foot to shoot me in the face with a laser, and it will make sense. So let me tell. Let me tell you the uh preamble to the story.

item.title: My Memorabilia Cabinets
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to walk you through my two glass cabinets of curio curios memorabilia, detris, from now, my 44th year in the game industry.

I’ve actually put things in there within the last month. People have been asking me to do this since pretty much I began the channel and to parap phrase: Chief wigam, what is your fascination with my forbidden closet of mystery? I get people want to see what’s in there. Um, for me, a lot of the stuff is just fun junk. I mean, some of it is really, you know, close to my heart, memorabilia from the games. Others are stuff I picked up online because I thought it was funny.

item.title: Wading In The Shallows
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about waiting in the shallows.

I’ll tell you what I mean by this. I actually have an alternate title which I’ll give you at the end. Um, waiting in the shallows is what I’ve said to people in the past, when I want to express that you can’t really know something, you can’t really understand a topic after just a casual involvement in it. This you’ve heard people talk about like armchair quarterbacks, and you know people like to give medical advice because they looked up something on WebMD.

item.title: Dice
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about dice.

You see, I did a video, well, from your point of view, I did a px video a few weeks ago. From my point of view, I did it a few days ago because when I did the PX 2024 video, which I’ll link below, I moved it to the front of the queue because pxs have just happened and I don’t do topical stuff very often. So it was like, well, let’s put it in the front of the queue. This video isn’t topical, so I’m going to the end of the queue. So, even though it’s only been a few days for me, it’s weeks for you, which should give you an idea of how big my queue is. I got a lot of requests on that video to talk about my dice because, I mentioned, I have a lot of dice. I have a bit of an addiction to dice. I’ve been ever since I got my first polyhedral set 45 years ago.

item.title: Channel Analytics (August 2024)
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Channel analytics.

Now, why am I talking about this? Well, I hit 160,000 Subs just a couple days ago — I’m filming this — at the end of August and a friend of mine saw me hit that and asked me about Channel analytics and I was like I don’t know what you’re referring to. He said: well, if you go into your YouTube, YouTu, Channel, you can dig around and pull up all these interesting numbers about your channel and I’m like, oh, that’s what that tab is for. I never looked at that tab. There’s like a, an earnings Tab and an analytics Tab and a copyright Tab and a um God. There’s a number of tabs that I either hardly look at or when I first started it, I opened them up and went huh, and then Clos them.

item.title: Polarizing Stories
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about polarizing stories.

I basically told a lot of Stories on this channel. Almost every one of my videos is just me telling stories and many of them have caused people to immediately fall into one of two camps. Um, at least the people who are commenting. Some people think that was a great story, you’re awesome, I’d love working with you. And then there’s other people going: what a horrible story. You’re terrible. You’re the whole reason I wouldn’t want to work in the game industry. I’m not here to comment on which one of those people, which of those are right or which of those are wrong. Um, I will say that my caution videos, which I’ll link below, really did that at a level I did not expect, mostly because they got picked up and um made viral by people with far more followers and subscribers than I have. Um, and people did reaction videos to them and whether or not they agreed with me or not, because they had such a big audience, there were a lot more people falling into those two camps of he’s horrible, he’s awesome and so kind of. What I want to talk about today is, uh, I found in my notes two stories that I looked back and forth between them. They weren’t near each other. Um, in fact, I’m going to strip away identifying characteristics so you don’t know what company it was at. You don’t know who these people were.

item.title: Good Lessons From A Bad Teacher
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about good Lessons From a Bad Teacher.

This is something that I talked about really briefly almost a year ago in a video called School versus self-taught, where I was talking about the pros and cons of learning at a University versus self — teing, you know, teaching yourself and also — I apologize, the dog is snoring up a storm. Um, in that video I talked about an experience I had with a really bad teacher my first year of engineering school, my undergraduate degree. I have a link below not only to the video, but to the time marker where I start talking about that, because I want to talk about it in more detail so you can really understand how bad it was. And then I want to tell you the lessons I walked away from that with and sort of how I think this going to help you approach things that happen to you that aren’t the best thing in the world but you can take away. Something good from it, so let’s dive into this a little in more detail.

item.title: Effect Of This Channel On Me
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the effect of this channel on me.

This has been. It was an interesting question. Um, I have another video about why a lot of people I know in the industry would never make a channel like this and you can go watch it. But this one was a response. This, video, is a response to a question I got asked by sherner, who asked: since you’ve started a YouTube channel, has it changed, your mind about any part of game development or reinforced your professional experiences? Yes, in fact, it’s done several of those, but I want to kick off the one that was the biggest surprise to me, which is a personal thing that had nothing to do with how I develop or how I think of my professional experiences, and that was very soon after making this channel. I realized that many people — not all — that’s the other thing.

item.title: Game Development Tunnel Vision
item.description: Sal SE, Tim, par gotcha. I bet you right now some of you are reaching for that YouTube translate button and some of you were thinking, whoa, Tim’s French accent is terrible, trust me, that’s been said. No, I’m doing this video about game development, tunnel vision, because it’s one of the things that occurred to me. I get a lot of questions about a lot of things and early on, when there were less of you, I would answer and say, oh, that’s not something I know or I can’t talk about it I did do a video on things I can’t or won’t answer, but since then there’s been a lot more questions that Encompass a lot more things, even about game development or tangentially related game development, that I literally cannot answer. I either don’t know it or I’m not qualified to answer.

item.title: Channel Memberships
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Channel memberships.

Yeah, I’m going to start Channel memberships on Canon games, and I know what you’re thinking. Why Channel memberships? Why? Now? I can answer that this channel has expanded Way Beyond where I thought it would be. I thought this channel would be me telling my stories to a few hundred, maybe a thousand of you.

Well, that’s not what happened. Currently — and this is, uh, late June of 2024 — currently I’m getting almost a million views a month and 100, almost 150,000 people have chosen to subscribe, so they’re getting the videos notifications, uh, when a new video comes up. I’m getting a lot of comments, several hundred a day, and a lot of those have questions in them that people want me to answer, and I’m trying my hardest. I’m keeping up reading all of them, but I can’t always answer all of them. I just don’t have the time, well, I’ve mentioned I do client work. One of my clients is winding. Down, the work for them is winding down, they’re.

item.title: Coming Out Stories
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk to you about some coming out stories that I have.

Yeah, I’m going to talk about being gay. This is the second of hundreds of videos I have where I’ve done that, but it’s Friday. It’s Friday, funay — right in the middle of June pride month, so I’m going to do it. If this makes you feel Oogie and you guys know who you are, just skip it and I’ll see you Monday. I’m not going to go on it, go over anything — game development today. So bye-bye anyway, so if you watched my other video about being a gay game developer, I didn’t come out until my 30s, which, just to put things in perspective, meant I was very closeted in the 80s and 90s when I was a teenager and a young man.

item.title: Pets
item.description:
Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about pets.

It’s Friday, Funday, and I’ve had a lot of people ask me two different questions. The first was: what?

What are elements of your game were very personal to you, and why are there so many dogs in your game? And the answer to both of those questions are pets, or, specifically, animals. Animals have been really important in my games. They’ve been really important throughout my life in my family.

item.title: My Own Struggles With Nuance
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Nuance again.

Now I already have two videos about nuance and trying to keep an open and fair mind about things, but this video is going to be about my own struggles with doing that, because it’s hard to do. I’ve had trouble with it I think a lot of you have trouble with it. A lot of my co-workers have trouble with It’s just.

item.title: Favorite Chocolate
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk to you about my favorite chocolate.

I did a chocolate video a few weeks ago. It was very well received. Um, a lot of people asked me — because my chocolate Blog has over 2,000 entries — what my favorite chocolates were. I’m happy to talk about chocolate and it’s Friday, so let’s do a fun Friday video. I’m going to start with my absolute favorite chocolate bar — just bar none. Then I’m going to talk about what I think the best brands are. I tried to make a top five but there was a four-way tie for the fifth slot.

item.title: I'm Naïve And Optimistic
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about how I’m naive and optimistic.

This may seem like a weird thing to say and some of you are going: oh yeah, I can tell I’ve watched this channel. I can tell, um others of you are going what, and let me just talk to you about I’m telling you this because I want you to know this, so this can be part of the lens through which you view my own channel. I’ve always been like this. I have been. I’ve stayed this way despite some of the negative stuff that’s happened, um, and I’ve also stayed this way despite of a lot of good things happening too. I’ve been told this by many people.

item.title: My 100k Award
item.description:
Hi everyone, it’s me Tim, well, this thing just [Music].

Arrived. What

item.title: My Family History Book
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my family history book.

This is a second book I’ve written. I’ve mentioned my Memoirs and I said I’m not going to publish it. This one I did make prints of and send them to a lot of people. I want to talk about it and then I want to tell you why I’m talking about it. So years ago I moved to California in 198- 7. I bought a house in 1995.

item.title: Digipick vs Sonic Screwdriver
item.description: Did you pick Sonic Screwdriver? Did you pick sonic?

item.title: Chocolate
item.description:
Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today’s Friday. So let’s talk about something fun.

That I will loop back to game development at the end, and what I want to talk about is chocolate. Do not underestimate how much I’m into chocolate. I love chocolate, I love eating it, I love talking about it I have a whole blog, which I’ll link to, which has thousands of entries, each one with a different ch, where I talk about who gave it to me, where it came from, what cacao percentage it is. Is it milk, dark chocolate, white chocolate, rose chocolate, which is the special Fourth Kind of chocolate, which is kind of like the plasma of the Chocolate World. At one point I could eat a bite of chocolate and tell you within 5% what the cacao percentage was, and sometimes I would tell you what country it was from. If it was single origin, I could tell you: oh, this is Ecuador, this is Madagascar, this is Ghana, because there would be a particular flavor to it.

item.title: Incorrect Assumptions
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about incorrect assumptions.

What I mean by that is there’s a lot of people watching this channel now and I’m really careful how I say things, but I’m reading all the comments. I I don’t always respond, in fact, I frequently don’t respond. I try to and I, if I have time and I have something that I can say that doesn’t require a big video, I try to answer, but I’m seeing a lot to I try to answer, but I’m seeing a lot of comments that make assumptions about what I’m saying that aren’t right, and I’ve done videos about watching my videos with nuance and, um, what it’s like to be a game director and what it’s like to be a programmer and what it’s like to be a designer. But this is something different. This is me saying one thing and a listener or Watcher, um, thinking I said something else and then responding to that, and sometimes I read a comment that’s so wrong. I don’t know how to respond, like the base assumptions of the comment are incorrect, so I don’t know what to say.

item.title: Challenge Yourself
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why you should challenge yourself and I’m going to cut to the Chase and tell you right now: success comes from challenging yourself.

There you can stop watching now, bye. Let me tell you, when I look at my own life, what I see, and then I can give you some advice on what you can do in your life. So I’ve said I’ve talked a lot about being in school and how I loved it. Um, I think part of that I was blessed when I was younger.

item.title: I Censored My Own Video
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about my only self-censored video on this channel.

Now I’ve talked about a lot of things on this channel. Most of them are positive and a lot of you have responded to that, and I appreciate it I have talked about some negative stuff and I do that mostly because I don’t want to paint this false Rosy picture of the game industry, because there are good things and there are bad things that happen, and that’s kind of what I want you to take away from. Almost every video is there good, there’s good stuff and there’s bad stuff.

item.title: Making A YouTube Channel, Part 2
item.description:
Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim, and this is part two of making a YouTube channel.

So I did part one back in August, I think it was. I’ll put a link below, and when this video comes out it will have been 6 months since I started doing this. There’s over 180 videos on this channel now because I’ve done one a day. And I say when this comes out it’s because when I was doing the videos where every day I wore a different shirt out of my boxed collection of video game shirts, some of those shirts were kind of stinky, so I’d film a video and then I’d switch to another like one of my shirts that I’d washed, and sometimes that day I’d film another video. And that way I got about two weeks ahead, which really helped, because I took a week-long trip during the period of making this Channel and no one noticed, because there was no, No Interruption of videos, because I had that buffer.

item.title: Characters Named Cain
item.description:
Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim. Yesterday’s video was kind of heavy, so today’s video I want to go a little lighter and I want to talk about all the freaking characters named Kane.

Growing up, of course, my last name’s Kan and I kept hearing my last name used in books, TV, movies, video games. Cut it out now. Sometimes it’s because it was biblical and they’re making an allegory, sometimes not. I had a teacher in high school who loved to ask when he’s talking to the class, Caine, are you able to answer? To answer this question, and of course I was a teenager at the time, so I had to go there.

item.title: Commenting On My Stories With Nuance
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Nuance again, particularly watching my videos and commenting on them.

Keep in mind you can comment on anything you want. I just kind of want to talk about some of the comments I’m seeing. There’s either enough of them that I felt like I should address them here, or they were so wild I didn’t want to call the person out for saying it, but I thought I’d address it now.

item.title: Extrovert or Introvert?
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer a question from echoness, who asks: Tim, which type of Personality are you?

Introvert or extrovert? Even though people can be super talkative about their passion, they can still be an introvert from the inside out. I’m curious: if you’re introvert, how do you handle, how do you navigate your work that requires endless communication?

Oh, econest, you have activated Tim Kaine story time because I have an answer for this, but it’s a little long, so go get coffee if you need it. The short answer is: I’m both and I can switch back and forth. That’s all you wanted to know. Tap out now.

item.title: Ambient Music
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about ambient music.

I brought this up in lots of videos. There’s a whole video about fallouts — music. And in my making of YouTube I talk about my big ambient collection. A lot of people asked about that, so let’s talk about that, because I have a very, very large ambient collection. I didn’t start listening to ambient until I was in college. In high school I was more about the new 80s wave that was coming: Depeche Mode, flux, Eagles, Tears for Fears, human League, cars — that’s what I tended to listen to in high school. When I got to college, I went to engineering school at the University of Virginia and I used to go study a lot with friends. I met there, um, and it was interesting. We’re all different, there was — uh, Donna was electrical engineering, Layla was Aerospace, I was computer engineering. They had an apartment off campus that was nice and quiet.

item.title: Making A YouTube Channel
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to digress from Game Dev and just talk a little bit about what

I’ve learned in the last three months about trying to make a YouTube channel, in particular this channel. Um, so I started off. I just wanted to share some stories and technical details about game development. I’ve been doing it for four decades. Now I’m two years into my fifth decade and I thought I had a lot of interesting stories and Tech to tail, tech details that might even make sense to non-programmers. But my number one thing was I tried writing these stories and apparently I don’t get the tone right. So I thought, okay, I’ll do videos, and I looked around for a way of reaching as many people as possible, but without a paywall. I did not want to make people pay to get at these, so that landed me on YouTube, which I already had a subscription to because I keep all my music.

item.title: The Origin of Recipes In My Games
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about all those recipes that appeared in a lot of my video games.

So for those of you who don’t want to hear personal information from me, you can just skip this. There’s not a lot of game development in it. You will be uninterested, so all of this started with stone keep and a pumpkin muffin recipe, but I’ll tell you a little bit about what led up to all that. So I grew up the youngest of five kids and I never really learned to cook at home. My mom would cook a lot of food. I had an older sister who cooked a lot.

item.title: Why I Don't Review Games
item.description: Foreign, It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why I don’t review other people’s games and, as a quick side note, why I haven’t been talking about The Outer Worlds very much, so real quick in the The Outer Worlds. I’m getting a lot of questions about it, but I’ve told you guys I’m working on The Outer Worlds too, so I’m a contractor with obsidian to give them consultancy on that game’s design. Many of your questions are things like: what do you wish wasn’t cut or what would you do different?

item.title: Being A Gay Game Developer
item.description:
Hi, it’s me, Tim. It is June 1st, happy pride month.

Let’s talk about being gay and being in the game industry. I got 40 years to cover four full decades. So this might be a little long. So the two long didn’t watch. The tldw is things are better. I was in the closet for the first half, not for the second half. So now let’s go through it decade by decade. So let’s start in the 80s. My first job was in 1981 at a little game company called Pegasus changed its name to cybron. I was in high school, totally in the closet. The 80s were when AIDS became a big thing. The news was constantly feeding stories about if you’re gay you’re going to die, and that scared teenager Tim luckily, the.

item.title: My Personal Information
item.description: Hey everyone. I made a video this morning about being gay in the game industry and several people have left comments basically saying, hey, I don’t need to know any personal information, just stick to game development stories. Well, if you felt the need to leave a comment like that on that video but not on the one about me being colorblind, my guess is, your problem isn’t with me talking about personal information, just something to think about. Happy Pride.

item.title: Listening To My Stories With Nuance
item.description:
Hi everyone, it’s Tim, so the last couple videos I made generated a lot of comments.

I read them. I wasn’t replying. I kind of knew what kind of comments they would generate, and I see some polarization happening where some people say, oh, I’m a victim, and other people are saying, hey, you accepted responsibility for the project, you know so bonuses or icing, you don’t deserve him. I get it, but what I want to talk about today — and this is going to sound ironic coming from a colorblind person, but I want to remind people that things aren’t as black and white in these stories as you make them out to be. These are real stories about real people.

item.title: My Colorblindness and Games
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk to you about color blindness and specifically my color blindness, which is a weird one, the weird form of it I want to talk to you about exactly what my color blindness is and then I want to talk about how it’s affected, how I play games and make games.

So I have a form of colorblindness. I think the total description is adult onset, Progressive, full spectrum color blindness. What this means is I had full color vision when I was a child, and it might, and as a teenager, but starting when I was 20.. I missed one of those ishi Hara color plates.

item.title: A Quick Note on Ads
item.description: So I just found out that some people are seeing ads in my videos. I did not think that ads would show up in unmonetized videos, but apparently they can be inserted. So what I’ve done is I turned on monetization, and immediately made all the ad skippable, and got rid of what are called mid-roll ads, which are ones that can show up in the middle of my videos. That’s the best I can do, except if I make videos that are, at least, it said, longer than eight minutes, but I tried it with some of my eight minute eight and a half minutes and it didn’t work. So it looks like it’s nine minutes or longer.

item.title: Introduction to "Cain on Games"!
item.description:
Hi there, my name is Timothy Kane and I’ve been a professional game developer for more than 40 years. Back in the 80s, I made a game called Grand Slam bridge and most recently I made an RPG called The Outer Worlds. In between, I made a lot of other games.

You may have heard Fallout. You may have enjoyed games from Troika games. I worked on MMOs for a while and even worked on a game that was one of the top grossing games from Kickstarter. I’m going to use this channel to tell stories about what game development has been like for the last 40 years and hopefully have some insights and some fun, so I hope you join me. Thank you, bye.
